title: Reported Speech: Quick Reference
intro: Use this sheet to convert direct speech to reported speech: tense backshift, pronoun/time changes, and core reporting patterns.

backshift.example: "I like it." -> He said he liked it.
"I am leaving." -> She said she was leaving.
"I have finished." -> He said he had finished.
"I saw him." -> She said she had seen him.
"I will help." -> She said she would help.
"I can swim." -> He said he could swim.
"I must go." -> She said she had to go.
"I may be late." -> He said he might be late.

time_place_shifts.reported: the next day / the following day

patterns.form: say (that) + clause / say to + person + (that) + clause
patterns.example: She said (that) she was tired. / She said to me (that) she was tired.
She told me (that) she was tired.
He asked if I was coming.
She told me to close the door. / He asked me to wait.

no_backshift: General truths often stay present: The teacher said water boils at 100 C.
If the situation is still true now, present can remain: She said she lives in Prague.
You can backshift for distance/formality, but keep one logic consistently.

common_errors.wrong: She said me that she was tired.
common_errors.right: She told me that she was tired. / She said to me that she was tired.

common_errors.wrong: He asked where do I live.
She told that she would come.
common_errors.right: She said that she would come. / She told me that she would come.

common_errors.wrong: He said me to close the window.
common_errors.right: He told me to close the window.
